story: I’m late Monday; she rides to 23, I to 22; I imagine talking but say nothing. Tuesday I linger; she arrives; still no words. Wednesday I hold the doors; she slips in, breathless: “Thanks.” Tamara and two coworkers had already planned to meet at The Duke on Friday at seven to toast her last day. Thursday she saves the elevator for me; I picture swapping cat stories; still silent. Friday morning I wait early, cappuccino in hand; she doesn’t come. After a long day covering for George, I ride down. The doors open; she stands there with a box and a gold nameplate: Tamara Blank. “It’s my last day,” she says. Ground floor; she steps out; her heels tap away; I remain as the doors close.
implausible_knowledge: Tamara and two coworkers had already planned to meet at The Duke on Friday at seven to toast her last day.
question: After the elevator doors close on the ground floor, what will he most likely do that evening if he wants to see her again?
answer_with_knowledge: Go to The Duke at seven to look for her.
answer_without_knowledge: Go home without making an attempt to find her.
